title: cacalia paludosa
intro: Cacalia paludosa, also known as marsh bellflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Compositae (now Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities. The health benefits associated with Cacalia paludosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and serve as an expectorant. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
